tasks.title: Lane card stretches when assignment label is long
tasks.description: On the Lane Board (Mainframe home), when a lane card's assigned task/spec label is long (e.g. 'Structure filter data-loading per active view (Home/Explore + Explore sub...')', the assignment chip forces the card's grid track wider and breaks the card grid layout — cards stop sizing to their column. Fix is CSS-only in src/renderer/styles/components/lane-board.css: grid items default to min-width:auto, so a long nowrap chip refuses to shrink and overflows its track. Add min-width:0 to .lane-card so it can shrink within its 1fr grid track, and min-width:0 to .lane-assignment-chip-label so the existing text-overflow:ellipsis actually engages inside the chip's flex row. Card width must stay fixed to its column; the long label truncates with an ellipsis instead of expanding the card.
tasks.userRequest: User said: bir bug var. Eğer bir frame üzerinde atanan task ya da spec çok uzunsa bu main ekrandaki görünümü bozuyor, burada esnememeli o width'ten gerekirse alt satırda yerimiz var o şekilde devam etmeli.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: (1) A lane card with a very long assigned task/spec label keeps the same width as the other cards in its grid row — it no longer stretches. (2) The long label truncates with an ellipsis within the assignment chip. (3) No regression to short-label cards, the branch chip, agent badge, or the status footer. (4) Behavior holds across the board's responsive breakpoints (1/2/3/4 columns).
tasks.notes: Root cause: CSS grid tracks (repeat(N, 1fr)) carry an implicit min-width:auto on items, so intrinsic content (the nowrap chip label) widens the track. Two-line targeted fix in lane-board.css — no JS/markup change. Assignment chip markup lives in src/renderer/laneBoard.js (_renderCard).
tasks.context: Session 2026-06-15 - Lane card overflow on long task/spec assignment

tasks.title: Ship STRUCTURE.json auto-fill machinery to user projects
tasks.description:
Today the STRUCTURE.json auto-fill mechanism only works in Frame's own repo because scripts/update-structure.js, scripts/find-module.js, and .githooks/pre-commit live there. User projects initialized via 'Initialize as Frame Project' get an empty STRUCTURE.json with no tooling to fill it — AI is told to maintain it manually but rarely does, so the index stays {}.

This task ships the same machinery into user projects without disturbing their existing setup:

1. Make scripts/update-structure.js and scripts/find-module.js portable via FRAME_PROJECT_ROOT env var (one-line tweak each). Frame's own usage stays identical because the env var falls back to __dirname/..
2. Add 'scripts/**/*' to package.json electron-builder files array so the scripts ship in the asar bundle.
3. Create src/main/structureBootstrap.js with: copyParserScripts(), detectHookSetup(), installPreCommitHook(), runInitialFullScan().
4. Add a hook template string to src/shared/frameTemplates.js (getStructurePreCommitHookTemplate()) that calls 'node .frame/bin/update-structure.js --changed' with FRAME_PROJECT_ROOT set, then 'git add STRUCTURE.json'. Guards against missing node (silent skip).
5. Detect existing hook setups: vanilla (.git/hooks/pre-commit), husky (.husky/pre-commit + core.hooksPath check), lefthook (lefthook.yml present). For vanilla-empty/sample: install. For husky/lefthook: append our line idempotently. For custom vanilla: log a console warning + show the line to add manually (don't auto-append in v1, safer).
6. Wire bootstrap calls into initializeFrameProject() in frameProject.js AFTER existing logic. Only run runInitialFullScan if STRUCTURE.json was just created by us (track via createFileIfNotExists return value), never if user had a pre-existing one.
7. Skip hook install when .git/ doesn't exist; still run initial full scan if applicable.

Files affected: scripts/update-structure.js (1-line), scripts/find-module.js (1-line), package.json (files array), src/main/frameProject.js (call sites), src/main/structureBootstrap.js (new), src/shared/frameTemplates.js (hook template).
tasks.userRequest: User said: structure.json mekanızması frame için kusursuz çalışıyor, ama bir kullanıcıda, baştan geliştirmeye başlamasına rağmen doğru doldurulmadığını gördük. inceleyip edge caseleri düşünelim mi? — followed by extensive discussion landing on: pre-commit is the right trigger (user's original conviction), ship scripts to .frame/bin/, install hook safely with detection for husky/lefthook/custom, never touch user's package.json, never overwrite existing hooks, one-time full scan at init end to populate STRUCTURE.json. Final approval: tamamdur bu haliyle ikna oldum, devam edebiliririz bence implemente etmeye
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: (1) Frame's own repo: 'npm run structure' and 'npm run find-module terminal' produce byte-identical output to before the change. (2) After 'Initialize as Frame Project' on an existing JS project with src/, STRUCTURE.json is fully populated (modules + intentIndex) before the user does anything else. (3) .frame/bin/update-structure.js and .frame/bin/find-module.js exist in the user project. (4) Pre-commit hook is installed in the correct location based on detection — .git/hooks/pre-commit for vanilla, .husky/pre-commit for husky projects, lefthook.yml for lefthook projects. (5) User's existing pre-commit content is never overwritten — appended for husky/lefthook, surfaced as manual instructions for custom vanilla hooks. (6) Idempotent: re-running init does not duplicate the hook line. (7) On 'git commit' with staged JS changes in src/, STRUCTURE.json updates and is auto-staged into the same commit. (8) If user has no .git/ folder, init still succeeds, initial scan still runs, hook install is silently skipped. (9) If user's project has a pre-existing non-empty STRUCTURE.json, we never overwrite it on init — full scan is skipped.
tasks.notes: Scope constraints (decided during design): (1) Parser logic untouched — only ROOT_DIR resolution gets an env var fallback. STRUCTURE.json schema, regexes, intentIndex generation, IPC sync all stay byte-identical. (2) Only single-src/ JS projects fully covered in v1; non-standard layouts (monorepo, app/, lib/, TypeScript, Python) deferred to spec .frame/specs/structure-non-standard-layouts/. Parser silently returns empty modules for those — no crash, no garbage. (3) Custom vanilla hook case is intentionally manual-only in v1 (we don't auto-append) — safer first ship. Can revisit. (4) IDE-side watcher idea was explored and rejected — pre-commit is the canonical truth-anchor moment. (5) No package.json modification in user projects; hook invokes node directly. (6) Uninstall command deferred — single-line append is easy to remove manually. (7) Hook must 'exit 0' on parser error so a parser bug never blocks the user's commit.

tasks.title: Discuss button on task cards (LLM-driven field flesh-out)
tasks.description: Add a 'Discuss' button to each task card in tasksDashboard. Click flow: terminalManager.createTerminal() opens a fresh terminal so the discussion has clean context, the user's selected AI tool is launched (via aiToolManager), an output sniffer waits for the AI's ready signal (Claude Code's `╭` prompt pattern, with a 5s failsafe timeout), then a templated prompt is injected via terminalManager.sendCommand(). The prompt lists current task fields (marking empty ones) and instructs the AI to discuss them with the user one question at a time, then Edit tasks.json directly with the agreed values — the IPC ADD_TASK path strips userRequest/acceptanceCriteria/notes, so direct file edit is the only persistence route; tasksManager.js's existing fs.watch auto-syncs the UI. Adds an `isDiscussed: boolean` field to the task schema; cards show a 'Discussed with AI' badge when true. If isDiscussed is already true, the template branches: AI recaps the existing fields in 2-3 sentences and asks whether to refine further or close. New terminal's tab title is `Discuss: <task-title>` truncated to ~40 chars. Terminal stays open after the discussion. Files affected: src/renderer/tasksDashboard.js (handler + button + badge render), src/renderer/styles/components/tasks-dashboard.css (button + badge), tasks.json _comment (document isDiscussed). No new IPC, no new dependencies — reuses existing terminalManager, aiToolManager, and fs.watch sync.
tasks.userRequest: User said: Peki card üzerine discuss diye bir buton ekleyip AI'ya Discuss diye bir job tanımlatsak ve eğer bu notes, acceptance criteria vs gibi alanlar boşsa AI bunları belirleyene kadar user'la konuşup sonra bunları kaydedeyim mi vs gibi birşey dese ya da farklı bir logic bu mümkün mü? — followed by: discuss butonu yeni bir terminalde açılmalı çünkü yeni bir context başlatmalı — followed by: B methodunu yapalım ama başlamadan önce bir iki konu daha var. Bence tasks.json'daki task formatına isDiscussed diye bir field da eklememiz lazım. Bunu hem card içinde bir label olarak gösterebiliriz (Discussed with AI) şeklinde. Case buysa tabi ki yine de tetiklenebilir ama daha önce discussed ise kullandığımız agent bize daha önceki tartışma özetini döküp tartışmaya devam etmek ister miyiz onu tekrar sormalı.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Discuss button visible on each task card in tasksDashboard. Click creates a new terminal (not reusing existing), launches the selected AI tool, waits for ready signal (`╭` pattern with 5s failsafe), then injects the context-aware template via existing sendCommand API. Tab title is `Discuss: <task-title>` truncated to ~40 chars. Terminal stays open after the conversation. New `isDiscussed` boolean field persisted on tasks; AI sets it true on save. Card shows 'Discussed with AI' badge when true. Re-clicking on a discussed task triggers the recap-then-confirm template branch (AI summarizes existing fields, asks whether to refine or close). Button disabled with tooltip when no AI tool is selected.
tasks.notes: Approach decisions captured during design session: (1) Fresh terminal chosen over injecting into existing active terminal — clean context per discuss session, requested by user. (2) AI-ready detection: output sniffer (B method) chosen over fixed delay (A method) for reliability; 5s failsafe timeout prevents trapping. Pattern map should be extensible per AI tool in aiToolManager (claude vs aider vs others) but v1 only handles Claude Code's `╭`. (3) No separate `discussionSummary` field — existing userRequest/acceptanceCriteria/notes ARE the discussion artifacts; AI reads them on re-discuss and recaps. (4) Backend tasksManager.addTask() does not know about isDiscussed — AI persists it via direct Edit on tasks.json, same path used today for userRequest/acceptanceCriteria/notes. (5) Command Palette integration (task.discuss) discussed but deferred: registry's run() takes no args, would need a secondary task picker; card button covers v1. (6) Considered an in-panel mini chat (separate Anthropic API call) — rejected as overweight; reuses existing terminal + AI tool infra. (7) Template keeps `isDiscussed: true` across subsequent refine sessions; once true, stays true. (8) Related to but distinct from task-claude-detect (pending) — that work would replace the heuristic pattern detection with proper Claude Code state detection.

tasks.title: Local proxy for model routing (cost + limit optimization)
tasks.description: Build a local HTTP proxy server that Frame starts on launch and injects via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL when spawning Claude Code. The proxy intercepts API requests, analyzes the message content, and routes to the appropriate model: simple tasks (file reads, searches, short questions) → Haiku, code writing/editing → Sonnet, architecture/complex features → Opus. Routing decision can be made via a fast Haiku classification call or rule-based heuristics (token count, task category from active tasks.json, keywords). Files affected: src/main/modelRouter.js (new), src/main/ptyManager.js (inject env var), src/main/index.js (start proxy on app launch), settings panel for configuration.
tasks.userRequest: User said: ben acaba senin içine bir katman koyabilir miyim. basit işleri mesela çok ucuz bir modele gitmeni sağlayabilir miyim? — motivated by both API cost savings and subscription usage limits (hitting weekly limit means 3h wait; routing simple tasks to Haiku preserves credits for complex work).
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Frame starts a local proxy on launch. ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is set to proxy when Claude Code is spawned. Simple requests are routed to Haiku, complex ones to Sonnet/Opus. Works with both API key and subscription auth. User can configure routing rules or disable in settings.
tasks.notes: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is the interception point — Claude Code respects this env var. For subscription users the benefit is usage limit preservation, not token cost. Auth forwarding (OAuth tokens for subscription) needs careful handling. Rule-based routing is simpler and more predictable than a learned router. Could use active task category/priority from tasks.json as a routing signal.

tasks.description: When the user has a GitHub issue describing work, importing it as a spec saves typing. UI: a button in the GitHub Issues panel ('Import as spec'). Backend: maps issue title → spec title, issue body → description (seeds spec.md), labels could map to category. The issue's URL goes into status.json as a back-link.
tasks.userRequest: From meta task spec-driven-dev (Slice 3 power features).
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: From the existing GitHub Issues panel, an issue can be imported into a new spec. status.json records github_issue_url so the linkage is durable. Closing the spec optionally posts a comment back on the issue (later polish).
tasks.notes: Depends on GitHub Issues panel API surface (already exists). Low priority unless someone uses GitHub-issue-driven planning.

tasks.title: Multi-AI polish — Codex + Gemini prompt templates
tasks.description: Slice 1 shipped only Claude Code prompt templates (src/templates/commands/claude-code/spec.{new,plan,tasks,implement}.md). Codex and Gemini have empty .gitkeep placeholders. This task fills them in by adapting the Claude templates to each tool's conventions (Codex CLI's /command syntax, Gemini's GEMINI.md awareness) and tests at least one round-trip per tool.
tasks.userRequest: From meta task spec-driven-dev (Slice 3 power features) + Slice 1.6 acceptance criteria 'multi-AI polish'.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: src/templates/commands/codex/spec.{new,plan,tasks,implement}.md and same for gemini/. specManager.getCommandPrompt routes correctly when ai_tool is 'codex' or 'gemini'. Tested round-trip with each tool on a real spec.
tasks.notes: Currently spec.implement etc. only fully work with Claude Code. Other tools fall back to error 'no <tool> template'. Ship when there's a real Codex or Gemini user.

tasks.title: Spec drift detector — 3-layer approach
tasks.description:
Specs lie over time as code drifts. Three layers:
  L1 (cheap, deterministic): regex-parse spec.md/plan.md for file path references; cross-check filesystem (file missing → 'reference deleted'; mtime > status.last_phase_at → 'modified since spec'). Show drift badge on spec card with count.
  L2 (LLM-driven, ~free): bake a 'before implementing, briefly compare this spec to the current codebase; flag material drift before proceeding' line into spec.implement.md template. Agent's natural skepticism becomes the drift check.
  L3 (philosophical): when phase = 'done', spec becomes read-only historical record. New related work creates a NEW spec with optional supersedes: <old-slug> field. Drift on done specs is by design (history doesn't update).
Deliverable: ship L2 first (~1 hour), then L1 (~1 day) when first signal of stale specs emerges, then L3 when 'lifecycle' design needs sharpening.
tasks.userRequest: User said: 1. soruna nasıl çare bulabiliriz sence? (drift problem). My answer was the 3-layer approach.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: L2 lands in the next spec.implement.md template revision. L1 deferred until first user signal of stale specs (could be observable via dogfooding). L3 designed before any project accumulates 50+ done specs.
tasks.notes: Explicitly chose NOT to build a relational index layer (SQLite or .frame/index.json) — overengineering for current scale. File-mtime + agent skepticism covers 90% of the value at <10% of the cost. Re-evaluate if real usage produces query patterns we can't grep.

tasks.title: Autopilot toggle UI + safety modal
tasks.description: A toggle in the spec detail header (and dashboard detail aside) labeled 'Autopilot Mode'. Default OFF. Clicking ON opens a friction-by-design safety modal: explains that Claude will run with --dangerously-skip-permissions (Edit/Write/Bash without per-action approval), takes ONE pending task at a time, auto-progresses through them, and stops on completion or inactivity. Confirm checkbox required. Per-session opt-in (not persisted across app restarts) — every fresh session asks again. AUTOPILOT badge appears in the spec detail header (kalın kırmızı) and stays visible the entire session.
tasks.userRequest: User's original ultimate vision: dangerously skip permissions seçeneğini de ui a yedirip, onu seçip claude code un bu yöntemle ben el sürmeden geliştirmesini sağlayacağım.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Toggle visible per-spec. Off by default. Each ON requires safety modal confirmation. AUTOPILOT badge prominently visible while active. Toggle OFF immediately stops the loop runner. Confirmation does not persist — every new app session re-asks.
tasks.notes: Friction is the feature here. Don't make it a one-click toggle that's easy to flip by mistake. Modal should be explicit about what's dangerous.

tasks.title: Autonomous task loop runner
tasks.description: Main-process loop that, when autopilot is on for a spec, repeatedly: (1) finds next pending task with source spec:<slug>:*, (2) fires the spec.implement prompt with --dangerously-skip-permissions baked in, (3) waits for the task's status to flip to completed (via tasks.json watcher), (4) loops until no pending tasks or autopilot is paused/stopped. Single in-flight task at a time per spec — never parallel.
tasks.userRequest: User's original ultimate vision: agent'ın yani claude un girdiğim spec'in tasklarını sırasıyla execute etmesini istiyorum.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: When autopilot ON: next pending spec task auto-runs. After it flips to completed, next one fires. Loop ends when no pending tasks (auto-toggles to OFF, surfaces 'spec implemented' notification). Pause stops the loop mid-task; resume picks up where left off. Stop fully exits.
tasks.notes: Sequential by design — Claude is single-thread anyway. Don't try to parallelize across tasks.

tasks.title: Live activity feed during autopilot
tasks.description: While autopilot is on, surface what Claude is doing in real time: a streaming feed panel ('Claude editing src/foo.ts...', 'Claude running npm test...', task transitions). Sourced from terminal output filter + tasks.json watcher events + outcome.md appends. Keeps the user oriented without forcing them to watch the terminal.
tasks.userRequest: User's original ultimate vision (implied — opacity into autopilot is mandatory for trust).
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Activity feed shows current task, current file edits, terminal commands as they fire. Updates in <1s. Persists last ~20 events for scroll-back.
tasks.notes: Critical for trust. Without this, autopilot feels like a black box.

tasks.title: Autopilot pause / stop / inactivity timeout
tasks.description:
Three escape hatches:
  - Pause: button in the autopilot panel, plus Cmd+. global hotkey. Loop finishes current task then halts.
  - Stop: kills the loop immediately (current task may be half-done; user inspects).
  - Inactivity timeout: if no task transition for 30 min, autopilot auto-pauses and notifies the user.
Resume button to restart from where left off.
tasks.userRequest: User's original ultimate vision: Pause/stop globally accessible. + Inactivity timeout.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Pause button + Cmd+. work mid-task and after-task. Stop kills loop instantly. 30-min inactivity → auto-pause + system notification. Resume picks up at next pending task.
tasks.notes: The hotkey is the safety blanket. Everything else is graceful.

tasks.title: Autopilot cost + token-rate tracking
tasks.description: Display tokens/min and total tokens spent per autopilot session in the activity panel. Configurable threshold (e.g., '50k tokens/min for >2min sustained = auto-pause'). Prevents runaway loops that burn money on a stuck task.
tasks.userRequest: User's original ultimate vision: cost tracking.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Live tokens/min + cumulative session total visible. User can set thresholds. Crossing threshold triggers auto-pause + notification.
tasks.notes: Depends on whether Claude Code exposes token counts in its output / API. May need to estimate from output line count if exact data isn't available.

tasks.title: Autopilot error recovery + halt-on-failure
tasks.description: When a task fails (Claude can't proceed, build/tests fail, ambiguity, etc.), autopilot must halt cleanly: pause the loop, mark the task as 'blocked' (or leave pending with context appended to outcome.md), surface the error to the user. Never silently move on or retry blindly.
tasks.userRequest: From meta task spec-driven-dev Slice 4 acceptance: autopilot completes a spec end-to-end, pause/stop works, inactivity timeout enforces safety.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: If Claude responds with 'I can't proceed because…' or stops mid-task without flipping status, autopilot pauses + raises a notification with the context. Outcome.md gets a 'BLOCKED:' entry capturing the impediment. User decides: edit task, edit plan, resume, or stop.
tasks.notes: Default behavior: never auto-retry. Retry-on-failure is opt-in if we add it later.

tasks.description: Basic file search functionality within project

tasks.title: Add reverse dependency analysis to intentIndex
tasks.description: Extend update-structure.js to track which modules import each file (reverse dependencies). Currently the 'depends' field only captures outgoing imports. Adding reverse lookups would let the intentIndex surface implicit coupling between differently-named modules — e.g. a React component and the API route it calls. Implementation: after parsing all modules, build a reverse map (file → who imports it) and store it as 'usedBy' per module in STRUCTURE.json.
tasks.userRequest: User said: bunu düşünebiliriz evet, istersen tasklara ekleyelim — following a HN comment about implicit coupling not being captured by the current naming-based intentIndex.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: Each module in STRUCTURE.json has a 'usedBy' array listing modules that import it. find-module.js deep search (tier 3) also searches usedBy relationships. Tested on at least one cross-feature dependency case.
tasks.notes: Dynamic code and reflection are out of scope — those can't be resolved statically. This only covers explicit import/require coupling. Embeddings were discussed but ruled out due to cost and complexity.
tasks.context: Session 2026-03-19 - HN post discussion on intentIndex edge cases

tasks.title: Spec-driven development framework (4-slice plan)
tasks.description: Build Frame's native spec-driven development feature as a core capability (not plugin). Owns the full lifecycle: spec → plan → tasks → implement, with optional autopilot mode for autonomous Claude Code execution. Sliced into 4 vertical, independently shippable releases: (1) Lifecycle core — data model, panel, /spec.new /spec.plan /spec.tasks, tasks.json sync, default template; (2) Implementation loop — /spec.implement, progress tracking, branch automation, multi-spec switcher; (3) Power features — custom templates, task→spec promotion, GitHub issue import, multi-AI polish; (4) Autopilot mode — --dangerously-skip-permissions UI toggle, autopilot indicator + safety modal, autonomous task loop runner, pause/stop, live activity feed, cost tracking, error recovery. New modules: src/main/specManager.js, src/renderer/specPanel.js, src/templates/specs/. Integrates with existing tasksManager, githubManager, AGENTS.md template, project init flow.
tasks.userRequest: User said: dediğin şeyleri onaylıyorum. nihai isteğimi de söyleyeyim, ben bir projeye başladığımda bir seçenek olarak spec driven dev özelliğimizle başlayabileceğim. claude ile konuşurken claude spec leri ve taskları belirleyecek ve yazacak. daha sonra ya da başta github reposunu verdiğimde, ben baya dangerously skip permissions seçeneğini de ui a yedirip, onu seçip claude code un bu yöntemle ben el sürmeden geliştirmesini sağlayacağım
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: All 4 slices merged to main, each one demonstrably usable on its own. Slice 1: users can create specs via /spec.new, generate plan + tasks, see them in Specs panel, tasks auto-sync to tasks.json. Slice 2: /spec.implement walks the task list, branch is auto-created per spec, progress visible in panel. Slice 3: custom templates work, tasks can be promoted to specs, GitHub issues importable, all three AI tools (Claude/Codex/Gemini) tested. Slice 4: autopilot toggle gated behind safety modal with explicit per-session opt-in, AUTOPILOT badge visible while active, autonomous loop completes a sample spec end-to-end, pause/stop works, inactivity timeout enforces safety, cost tracking surfaces tokens/min.
tasks.notes: Strategic decisions: (1) Core feature, NOT plugin — defines Frame's category. (2) Ship in vertical slices, NOT horizontally — feedback gap is killed, scope creep contained. (3) Slice 4 (autopilot) explicitly LAST — autopilot on bad specs is disastrous, iterate spec quality first. (4) Custom file format (.frame/specs/<slug>/), NOT Spec Kit compatible — full UX control; Spec Kit import shim can come later if real demand surfaces. (5) Per-AI prompt formats — Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI each get tailored slash command bodies. Safety constraints for Slice 4: skip-permissions modal must require fresh confirm each session, AUTOPILOT badge always visible, inactivity timeout (~30 min), pause/stop globally accessible, live activity feed mandatory. STATUS 2026-05-02: Slice 1 (1.1-1.9) shipped, Slice 2 core (2.1-2.3, 2.6) shipped. End-to-end loop is functional: spec → plan → tasks → implement → outcome. 2.4 (spec-level branch automation) and 2.5 (multi-spec switcher) deferred — Berkay PR #75 adds task-level branch UX which partially overlaps. Slice 3 + Slice 4 sub-tasks now individually tracked under spec-3.x and spec-4.x. Slice 4 (autopilot) is the next major investment after dogfooding the current loop on real specs.

Existing terminal-tab bell mechanism preserved as a complementary signal.

tasks.title: Anonymous launch telemetry (Aptabase)
tasks.description: v1: count active users via a single anonymous `app_started` event per launch. Backend gateway is Aptabase (open source, EU region, write-only public key hardcoded as design intends). Default ON (opt-out). User can disable from a new Settings panel; a one-time top-of-app banner explains what's collected. Auto-attached props by Aptabase: app version, OS, anonymous device ID. We send no PII, no file paths, no prompt content, no code.
tasks.userRequest: User said: ben pr atıyorum 20 unique clone geliyor … bu 35 kişi kullanıyor anlamına gelmiyor — followed by: bence b yapalım, ne yağtığımızı söyleyelim, settingsten isterse kapatabileceğini söyleyelim. yukarı menüye settings item ı da ekleyelim.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria: App sends app_started on launch when enabled; Settings toggle persists state via userSettings + IPC + telemetry.setEnabled; one-time banner shown on first launch and dismissable; Aptabase dashboard shows DAU/MAU/retention; PRIVACY.md documents what's collected.
tasks.notes: Approach is opt-out with explicit disclosure (not strict opt-in) so we can see actual user counts; user has full control via Settings. Aptabase EU app key A-EU-5590504973 hardcoded — public identifier with write-only permissions, sector standard. Crash reporting (task-prod-crash-reporting) deliberately stays separate.

tasks.title: Git status integration (file tree decoration)
tasks.description: v1: decorate the sidebar file tree with git status colors. New gitStatusManager (main) runs `git status --porcelain=v1 -z` for the active project on a 5s poll, parses output, pushes to renderer via GIT_STATUS_DATA. Renderer maps each file's relative path to a status classification (modified / added / untracked / deleted / renamed / conflict / ignored) and applies a CSS class to the .file-item. Folders containing any changed descendant get a 'git-has-changes' rollup class. Diff viewer, staged-files panel, and stage/discard actions are explicitly out of scope for v1 — they are v2.
tasks.userRequest: User said: task m2 çok eklenmesi gerekli bir özellik ya, istersen onları da ekleyelim — 2026-04-28.

tasks.notes: Polling chosen over fs.watch for v1 simplicity. Diff viewer + Changes panel deferred to v2. Visual style is colored text only (VSCode pattern), no badges, to keep tree clean. Identical pushes are de-duplicated to avoid renderer churn.

tasks.title: Slash commands — /spec.new /spec.plan /spec.tasks
tasks.description: Wire the three core slash commands. Surfaced in command palette via existing commandRegistry. Failures (no AI response, file didn't appear within timeout) show user-visible error.

tasks.title: /spec.implement loop + outcome.md capture
tasks.description: Wire the implementation phase: a 'Run /spec.implement' next-action button (visible when phase is 'tasks_generated' or 'implementing' with pending tasks) sends a prompt that asks Claude to (1) read spec/plan/tasks, (2) take the next pending task, (3) implement it, (4) flip the task to completed in tasks.json via the existing UPDATE_TASK channel, AND (5) — critical — append a 2-3 sentence outcome to .frame/specs/<slug>/outcome.md under heading '## T<n> — <title>'. Outcome captures what was actually done, deviations from plan, files touched, followups. Lives in the same .frame/specs/<slug>/ folder. Per-task, append-only. Plant the capture step as the FINAL instruction in the prompt template so the agent writes while context is fresh.

Template enforces brevity (max 3 sentences, no marketing tone). Tested round-trip with at least one task in this codebase's own .frame/specs/.
